channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats elect Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as their national chair
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Democrats on Saturday elected Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as the national chair, turning to a low-profile Midwestern political operative to coordinate their resistance to Donald Trump’s presidency.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Federal Reserve adviser indicted and arrested for alleged espionage in dealings with China
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested a former Federal Reserve senior adviser for allegedly giving inside economic information to China.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Where Trump's Cabinet nominees stand in Senate confirmation process
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Tulsi Gabbard and Kash Patel testify before Senate committees on Capitol Hill on Thursday as urgency builds to confirm President Donald Trump’s Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats elect Minnesota's Ken Martin as DNC chair on first ballot
item.description: Ken Martin of Minnesota is the new chair of the Democratic National Committee. Members of the DNC elected Martin on the first ballot in a vote outside of Washington D.C.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Woman who wanted to kill GOP leaders said she was inspired by CEO's slaying, prosecutors say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Massachusetts woman who told police that she went to the U.S. Capitol to kill members of President Donald Trump's cabinet said she was influenced by Luigi Mangione, the man charged with fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, prosecutors said in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE announces more than $1B in savings after canceling 104 federal DEI contracts
item.description: Two dozen federal agencies reported a collective $1 billion in savings from the elimination of DEIA/DEI offices and roles within their bureaucracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets FAA diversity efforts in plane crash probe despite no evidence they played any role
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump wasted little time this week trying to assign blame for the nation’s deadliest air disaster in more than two decades. Among his chief targets: An FAA diversity hiring initiative he suggested had undermined the agency’s effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, ALI SWENSON and ALEXANDRA OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official travels to Venezuela in push for Maduro regime to take back Tren de Aragua gang members
item.description: Ric Grenell has traveled to Venezuela to meet with socialist dictator Nicolas Maduro and demand that he accept deported criminals from the U.S. and release American hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois governor blocks pardoned Jan. 6 rioters from state jobs
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker (D) said he would block pardoned Capitol rioters from serving in local government positions, citing a state law ordinance, according to a Thursday letter obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Congested Reagan National site of several previous close calls
item.description: The complex airspace around Washington, D.C., has resulted in at least three near misses in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The DNC's outgoing chair says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Democratic National Committee prepares to elect a new chair, its departing leader says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in the 2024 race.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel grilled on potential Trump pressure, past comments at contentious hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was at the center of a contentious confirmation hearing Thursday, facing intense grilling from Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee about his past comments and allegiance to Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says Trump’s interest in Greenland and Panama Canal is legit and driven by China concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio says President Donald Trump’s desire to acquire Greenland and retake control of the Panama Canal is driven by legitimate national security interests stemming from growing concerns about Chinese activity and influence in the Arctic and in Latin America
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The effects of the Trump administration’s efforts to roll back DEI initiatives
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the steps President Donald Trump has taken to dismantle the federal government’s diversity, equity and inclusion programs and how some private companies have responded.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump orders Education, Labor and other departments to enhance school choice
item.description: The president directed several departments to submit plans for using federal funds to expand school choice.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
